methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: AI pressure on Richmond's young workforce without accompanying upskilling infrastructure signals shallow anxiety rather than deep capability development, particularly if workers feel displaced rather than empowered by automation.

signals.title: AI trainer says workers must lead technology, not follow it - WDBJ7
signals.signal_reading: AI training emphasizing worker agency ('lead technology, not follow it') in Southwest Virginia signals potential for distributed, deep capability if it moves beyond awareness workshops to hands-on peer learning networks that build actual AI fluency.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-backed community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia demonstrates distributed capacity-building infrastructure that could seed peer networks for AI adoption beyond metro concentration zones, contingent on program depth and local ownership.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - news.vcu.edu
signals.signal_reading: VCU partnership with AI Ready RVA concentrates educational innovation and institutional resources in Richmond Metro, potentially deepening capability in the capital region but creating replication challenges for less-resourced Virginia communities.

signals.title: Hampton Roads multiple listing service introduces AI tool to personalize home searches - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: Platform-delivered AI tools for real estate searches in Hampton Roads exemplify shallow but distributed adoption—users gain immediate utility through vendor solutions without developing underlying AI literacy or customization capability.
